calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-24-2022 Off Calendar

Preliminary Injunction
Off Calendar May-25-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances by any attorney, any party or any interested party.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

HEARING RE: PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION is OFF CALENDAR, Notice of Settlement on file. Judge Charles F. Haines, Clerk: N. Wong, Not reported (D501)

Preliminary Injunction
Off Calendar May-11-2022 Continued To May-25-22 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 501
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Warner Berry, Esq. 415-359-5743 for Plaintiff.
Charlie Stein, Esq. 818-661-2420 for Defendant (by Zoom).

Hearing is held. RULING - The matter is Discussed. Line number 5 (598916), 16 (667352) and 17 (667353) which are related cases, are called simultaneously. Parties report they are still working on a settlement. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

Plaintiff's Motion for Preliminary Injunction.

Continued to 5/25/22 at 9:30am in Dept. 501. Notice is waived. The Court states the stay is in effect.

Judge: Charles Haines, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (501/CFH)

Preliminary Injunction
Off Calendar Apr-27-2022 Continued To May-11-22 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 501
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:

Patrick J. Wingfield; Murphy Pearson Bradley & Feeney; 415-788-1900; appearing in person on behalf of plaintiffs Laurence Nasey, et al.
Charlie Z. Stein; Davidovich Stein Law Group LLP; 818-661-2420; appearing via Zoom on behalf of defendant's Fell Holdings LLC and Stanyan Holdings LLC.

Defendants' counsel contests the court's tentative ruling. The Amended Declaration of Eli Tabak in Support of Opposition is discussed. Plaintiffs' counsel states that mediation is scheduled for Friday, April 29, 2022. Defendants' counsel states that his clien't corporate filings are were submitted to the Secretary of State were filed at the end of March, and that they are still pending and filed with the date of submission once processed. After reviewing the pleadings and listening to oral arguments the Court rules as follows:

PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION is continued to May 11, 2022. Both sides waive notice.

Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not Reported. (D501)
